content.full_text:
October 6, 2005

Pamela Lawton Wilson
Sullivan Wertz McDade & Wallace
945 Fourth Avenue
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-05-101a

Dear Ms. Wilson:

This letter is to clarify the advice provided to you in Advice Letter No. A-05-101 on behalf of the San Diego Association of Realtors ("SDAR") regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

You requested advice concerning the reporting of costs and proceeds associated with the political fundraising portion of a golf tournament held for members and guests of SDAR. We advised you that any costs incurred by SDAR related to the political fundraising portion of the event would be considered nonmonetary contributions from SDAR to its sponsored committee.

Although our letter cited the exception to the definition of "contribution" in regulation 18215(c)(16) for establishment and administration costs paid by a sponsor for its sponsored committee, we did so for informational purposes only. When fundraising costs are paid by a sponsor for its sponsored committee, the payments are considered nonmonetary contributions to the committee, and not part of the costs for "establishment and administration" of the committee.2

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

2 That portion of the Jacobs Advice Letter, No. A-03-208, which similarly cites regulation 18215(c)(16) is also informational only.

If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

LM:rd
I:AdviceLtrs\05-101a
